questions.question: In which of the following Indian states does the Jagar dance form has its primary roots?
Which folk dance of Himachal Pradesh was recognised in the Guinness Book of World Records (2015) for having the largest number of participants performing simultaneously at a single venue?
The initial form of Odissi dance was performed by women serving in temples. What was their traditional designation?
Which of the following is a tribal dance form of India?
Which of the following terms is used to refer to the designated female devotees who enact the Lai Haraoba dance in Manipur, India?
The Karakattam (Karagattam) dance, known for its unique balancing of pitchers, is primarily prevalent in which of the following states of India?
Which of the following classical dance forms is known for storytelling through facial expressions and hand gestures, and comes from Tamil Nadu?
Yakshagana is a folk theatre form from which of the following states of India?
Jhumur, a traditional folk dance, originated in which of the following Indian states?
____________ is a classical dance form characterised by elaborate facial makeup, colourful costumes, and themes from Hindu epics, mainly performed by male dancers.
Who among the following is a famous Indian classical dancer known for popularising Bharatanatyam globally?
Which of the following classical theatre forms is associated with Karnataka and features dance-drama with colourful costumes?
Kathak is a classical dance form from which of the following regions of India?
Vedantam Prahlada Sarma, born in Andhra Pradesh in 1923, was initiated into which of the following Indian classical dance forms?
